objective: Deliver a runnable Electron 43 and Vue 3 desktop shell for the M07 avatar stage. The local packaged renderer must have Node integration disabled, Chromium sandbox and context isolation enabled, and access only a narrow typed preload API whose main-process handlers call the existing 127.0.0.1 control plane. The shell must display real avatar and safety status, allow labeled manual preview/reset/mute/emergency controls, and clearly retain the repository-original code-native fallback while VRM asset selection remains pending.
non_goals: Do not load remote web content, use webview, enable Node integration, expose ipcRenderer directly or provide a generic IPC/fetch/file/shell bridge.
Do not read SQLite, Qdrant, model files, provider objects, environment secrets or arbitrary filesystem paths from the renderer.
Do not add a VRM/Live2D/motion asset or claim that the code-native fallback is a VRM.
Do not duplicate conversation, memory, safety or avatar business policy in Vue.
Do not add an installer, auto-updater, code signing or public network binding in this slice.
Do not run packaging, repeat, dropped-frame or soak release gates during fast development.
contracts: BrowserWindow loads only repository-built local files with nodeIntegration false, contextIsolation true, sandbox true and webSecurity true; popup and unexpected navigation are denied.
Preload exposes one frozen window.hinaDesktop object with named methods only; ipcRenderer, send, invoke, shell, filesystem and generic channel names never cross into the renderer.
Main-process IPC handlers use a fixed operation-to-loopback-route allowlist and reject unknown operations, unexpected cue fields, non-owner cue sources and non-loopback control-plane base URLs.
Vue renderer contains presentation state only and never imports Electron, Node built-ins, database/model packages or runtime provider modules.
Desktop status is real control-plane data or an explicit offline error; it is never replaced with canned avatar, safety, latency or resource values.
Manual preview remains labeled manual-preview and uses owner.console; mute and emergency controls call the existing safety authority.
The desktop visual is covered by the repository-original asset manifest and reports vrmLoaded false until a separately licensed asset and adapter are approved.
allowed_dependencies: electron 43.2.0 (MIT), pinned as a development dependency.
vue 3.5.40 (MIT), pinned as the renderer dependency.
vite 8.1.5 and @vitejs/plugin-vue 6.0.8 (MIT), pinned for local build only.

typescript 6.0.3 (Apache-2.0), vue-tsc 3.3.8 and @types/node 26.1.1 (MIT), pinned for type checking/build only. TypeScript 7 is excluded because vue-tsc 3.3.8 cannot resolve its non-exported compiler subpath.
Existing Hina loopback control-plane HTTP endpoints; no new network service.
acceptance_criteria: Dependency lock and third-party provenance record exact versions, source repositories, licenses and that no upstream source file was copied.
Type checking and production build pass for Electron main/preload and Vue renderer.
Static security tests prove context isolation, sandbox, disabled Node integration, local-file loading, denied popup/navigation and the absence of generic IPC exposure.
Control-client unit tests prove only fixed loopback status/cue/reset/safety operations are allowed and response/error text is bounded.
Vue stage visibly distinguishes offline, runtime, manual-preview and tts-playback modes and reports code-native fallback with vrmLoaded false.
Desktop smoke launches the built local renderer in a hidden Electron window against the running loopback control plane and exits cleanly without a renderer crash.
Root fast startup behavior and existing Dev Console remain unchanged.
README documents start:desktop as a real app command and explains that the control plane must be running.
required_test_commands: uv run --frozen python -m unittest discover -s tests/governance -p "test_*.py"
